_comment: Template redox configuration for real redox-active molecules.
Use this file after adding matching residues to the simulation PDB/topology.
The current start_with_electrodes.pdb contains ECA/DMC/LiA/PF6 only, so these
species will not be sampled until the structure actually includes them.
These values are literature-inspired starting estimates for workflow calibration.
They are appropriate as templates, not as final production thermochemistry.

metadata.note: voltage_v is total cell voltage vs vacuum; CPF uses cathode=+V/2 and anode=-V/2

_comment_bq: 1,4-Benzoquinone (BQ), a real reversible organic redox molecule.
Typical first reduction in aprotic electrolyte is around -2.0 V vs Li/Li+
which corresponds to about -3.4 V vs vacuum with a +1.4 V offset.
The second reduction is much more negative and may fall near or outside the
present voltage window depending on electrolyte stabilization.

_comment_aq: Anthraquinone (AQ) template for a second real quinone redox probe.
AQ is generally easier to reduce than BQ in many aprotic media and is a
common organic battery redox motif. Tune these values against your own DFT
or experimental half-wave potentials before production use.
